name: Veils
description: Four-channel DC-coupled VCA with per-channel response curve, offset/boost, and normalled mixer. 2020 revision adds LED meters and selectable linear/exponential per channel.

controls.name: Level 1–4
controls.description: Per-channel gain knob. Also acts as CV offset when no input patched, scaling the CV attenuverter function.
controls.detail: 0–unity · gain up to ~4x (12 dB) · LED tracks output

controls.name: Response 1–4
controls.description: Per-channel response curve knob between exponential and linear CV tracking.

controls.name: CV Attenuverter 1–4
controls.description: Scales and inverts each channel's CV input when patched.
controls.detail: CCW: invert · noon: zero · CW: unity

inputs.name: CV 1
inputs.description: Channel 1 CV input, scaled by its attenuverter.

inputs.name: CV 2
inputs.description: Channel 2 CV input, scaled by its attenuverter.

inputs.name: CV 3
inputs.description: Channel 3 CV input, scaled by its attenuverter.

inputs.name: CV 4
inputs.description: Channel 4 CV input, scaled by its attenuverter.

outputs.name: OUT 1
outputs.description: Channel 1 direct output. Breaks that channel out of the mix normalling.

outputs.name: OUT 2
outputs.description: Channel 2 direct output. Breaks channel 2 out of the mix.

outputs.name: OUT 3
outputs.description: Channel 3 direct output. Breaks channel 3 out of the mix.

outputs.name: OUT 4 / MIX
outputs.description: Sum of all unbroken channels. With nothing patched in OUT 1–3 this is the full 4-channel mix; patching OUT 1–3 removes those channels from the mix.

behaviors.name: Normalled mixer
behaviors.description: Channels 1–3 internally sum into channel 4's bus. The MIX output delivers the combined signal. Patching any of OUT 1–3 removes that channel from the sum — no extra mixer needed for a 4-input setup.

behaviors.name: Per-channel response curve
behaviors.description: Each channel independently selectable between exponential (ear-matched for audio) and linear (artifact-free for CV). Set channels 1–2 linear for CV duties and 3–4 exponential for audio in a single Veils.

behaviors.name: Gain above unity
behaviors.trigger: Level knob past noon or high CV
behaviors.description: Each VCA can boost up to +12 dB for recovering signal after passive attenuation, hot-rodding oscillators, or deliberately clipping into the soft-saturation output stage for warmth.

behaviors.name: Soft clipping on MIX
behaviors.description: MIX output soft-saturates rather than hard-clips when channels combined exceed headroom. Useful as a deliberate drive stage — push inputs past unity for smooth analog compression character.

behaviors.name: DC-coupled CV processing
behaviors.trigger: CV signal patched through any channel
behaviors.description: All four channels pass DC, so Veils doubles as a four-channel CV attenuverter/processor: IN accepts CV, CV jack scales it, Level offsets it, MIX sums multiple CV streams.

behaviors.name: LED level meters
behaviors.description: 2020 revision adds bright per-channel LEDs tracking output amplitude. Useful as a diagnostic confirming VCA action, envelope shape, and signal presence at a glance.

patchIdeas.name: Four-voice submixer
patchIdeas.patch: Four voices patched into IN 1–4 · levels balanced by knobs · envelope on CV 1 shapes voice 1 · OUT 4 is the summed mix with soft saturation for glue
patchIdeas.diagram:
Veils:
* CH 1-4: Level at unity

- VCO 1 (Out) -> Veils (IN 1)
- VCO 2 (Out) -> Veils (IN 2)
- VCO 3 (Out) -> Veils (IN 3)
- VCO 4 (Out) -> Veils (IN 4)
- Envelope (Out) >> Veils (CV 1)
- Veils (OUT 4) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Drum bus with sidechain
patchIdeas.patch: Kick → IN 1, Snare → IN 2, Hats → IN 3 summed into MIX · bass → IN 4 broken out via OUT 4 · inverted kick envelope into CV 4 ducks bass on every kick hit
patchIdeas.diagram:
Veils:
* CH 4: Response CW (linear)

- Kick (Out) -> Veils (IN 1)
- Snare (Out) -> Veils (IN 2)
- Hats (Out) -> Veils (IN 3)
- Bass (Out) -> Veils (IN 4)
- Kick Env (Out) >> Veils (CV 4) // inverted via attenuverter CCW, ducks bass
- Veils (OUT 4) -> Drum Bus (In)

patchIdeas.name: Stereo VCA + CV attenuators
patchIdeas.patch: Channels 1–2 linear for stereo audio VCA (L/R with shared envelope mult) · channels 3–4 exponential for sculpting two CVs · one Veils covers stereo amp duties and two CV processors
patchIdeas.diagram:
Veils:
* CH 1-2: Response CW (linear)
* CH 3-4: Response CCW (expo)

- Synth (L) -> Veils (IN 1)
- Synth (R) -> Veils (IN 2)
- Envelope (Out) >> Veils (CV 1)
- Envelope (Out) >> Veils (CV 2)
- LFO (Out) -> Veils (IN 3)
- S&H (Out) -> Veils (IN 4)
- Veils (OUT 1) -> Output (L)
- Veils (OUT 2) -> Output (R)
- Veils (OUT 3) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Veils (OUT 4) >> VCO (FM)

patchIdeas.name: Soft-saturation drive
patchIdeas.patch: Bass VCO into IN 1 · Level pushed past unity · overdriving into OUT 4's soft clipping adds analog warmth/tube-style grit · level CV from envelope adds dynamic drive
patchIdeas.diagram:
Veils:
* CH 1: Level ~3 o'clock (boost)

- Bass VCO (Out) -> Veils (IN 1)
- Envelope (Out) >> Veils (CV 1)
- Veils (OUT 4) -> Filter (In) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Four-channel CV attenuverter bank
patchIdeas.patch: No audio patched · four CV sources into IN 1–4 · knobs set offsets · CV jacks trim per-channel depth · summed modulation on OUT 4 for complex motion on a single destination
patchIdeas.diagram:
Veils:
* CH 1-4: Response CW (linear)

- LFO 1 (Out) >> Veils (IN 1)
- LFO 2 (Out) >> Veils (IN 2)
- S&H (Out) >> Veils (IN 3)
- Envelope (Out) >> Veils (IN 4)
- Veils (OUT 4) >> Filter (Cutoff) // summed complex CV

patchIdeas.name: Per-voice amp envelope with shared VCA
patchIdeas.patch: Four voice VCOs into IN 1–4 · four independent envelopes into CV 1–4 · each voice broken out via its own OUT for per-voice panning/processing · MIX remains available if any left unpatched
patchIdeas.diagram:
Veils:
- VCO 1 (Out) -> Veils (IN 1)
- VCO 2 (Out) -> Veils (IN 2)
- VCO 3 (Out) -> Veils (IN 3)
- VCO 4 (Out) -> Veils (IN 4)
- Env 1 (Out) >> Veils (CV 1)
- Env 2 (Out) >> Veils (CV 2)
- Env 3 (Out) >> Veils (CV 3)
- Env 4 (Out) >> Veils (CV 4)
- Veils (OUT 1) -> Pan 1 (In)
- Veils (OUT 2) -> Pan 2 (In)
- Veils (OUT 3) -> Pan 3 (In)
- Veils (OUT 4) -> Pan 4 (In)

patchIdeas.name: Complex LFO via summed CV
patchIdeas.patch: Slow sine into IN 1 · fast sine (audio rate) into IN 2 · triangle at medium rate into IN 3 · S&H steps into IN 4 · each attenuverted differently · MIX emits rich non-repeating modulation for filter FM
patchIdeas.diagram:
Veils:
* CH 1: Level ~unity · Response linear
* CH 2: Level low · Response linear
* CH 3: Level medium · Response linear
* CH 4: Level low · Response linear

- LFO Slow (Out) -> Veils (IN 1)
- LFO Fast (Out) -> Veils (IN 2)
- LFO Mid (Out) -> Veils (IN 3)
- S&H (Out) -> Veils (IN 4)
- Veils (OUT 4) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Audio-rate amplitude modulation
patchIdeas.patch: Carrier VCO into IN 1 · fast LFO or second VCO at audio rate patched into CV 1 with attenuverter at unity · Level near unity · OUT 1 delivers ring-mod-style AM sidebands
patchIdeas.diagram:
Veils:
* CH 1: Response CW (linear)

- VCO Carrier (Out) -> Veils (IN 1)
- VCO Mod (Out) >> Veils (CV 1) // audio-rate AM
- Veils (OUT 1) -> Filter (In) -> Output (In)
